segments.s584-g129: همی داشتش is a continuous/past imperfect construction: همی marks ongoing action and داشت (with the enclitic object -ش) means 'he was holding/kept it.' چون introduces a simile ('like/as'), followed by the noun phrase یکی تازه سیب ('a fresh apple'), where یکی is an indefinite article-like word and تازه modifies سیب.
segments.s584-g130: که here introduces a purposive/result clause ('so that/that'), از باد is an ablative ('from/by the wind'), ناید is a negative optative/potential ('may not / should not come'), بروبر is an adverbial/qualifier meaning 'head‑on/face‑to‑face/directly', and نهیب is a noun meaning a sudden striking blow/roar — together: 'so that no wind may come and strike it directly.'
segments.s584-g131: به منذر marks the indirect object ('to Munḏir'), چنین گفت is a fixed phrase 'thus said,' روزی is a temporal adverb here ('one day'), and جوان is the subject 'the young man' (Persian allows the temporal phrase and verb to precede the subject).
segments.s584-g132: که‌ای is a poetic/vocative particle equivalent to 'O' when addressing someone; مرد is the addressee 'man' (here 'sir'/'man of …'); باهنگ is an epithet 'melodious' or 'of sweet speech', و is 'and', روشن‌روان is a compound epithet meaning 'bright‑soul' or 'light‑spirited.'
segments.s584-g133: چنین بی‌بهانه is an adverbial phrase 'thus, without cause/unprovoked'; همی adds continuous aspect ('always/continually'); داریم is present plural 'we have/hold' (here meaning 'we keep/maintain such a state').
segments.s584-g134: زمانی functions adverbially ('at times'/'sometimes'). به تیمار is a prepositional phrase 'to care/into care', and نگذاریم is the negative present-subjunctive/indicative 'we do not allow/entrust' (ن- + گذاریم), so the clause means 'sometimes we do not entrust (it) to care.'
segments.s584-g135: همی marks habitual/continuous aspect ('whenever/always'); هرک is an indefinite relative 'whoever/whomsoever'; بینی is second‑person singular present 'you see' (here 'you look/you behold'); تو is the explicit pronoun 'you'; اندر جهان is the locative 'in the world.'
segments.s584-g136: دلی نیست is an existential negation 'there is no heart' (دلی = 'a heart / heartfelt nature'); اندر جهان repeats the locus 'in the world'; بی‌نهان is an adjective 'without concealment / unhidden' — together meaning 'there is no open-hearted person in the world.'
segments.s584-g153: چو is a temporal conjunction here meaning "when." بشنید is a simple past active verb "he heard." منذر is a personal name (Munḏir) and is the subject of بشنید. ز + خسرو marks the source/author of the speech ("from Khosrow" / "Khosrow's"), and سخن is the direct object meaning "speech/words."
segments.s584-g154: This clause reports the reaction. The sequence is somewhat compressed: برو here is an archaic/poetic contraction (originally بر او = "to him"), so it does not mean the literal verb "go"; آفرین is the exclamation "bravo/afarin," and کرد is the past tense verb "did/uttered/made." مرد کهن is the nominative appositive identifying the speaker as "the old man."
segments.s584-g155: بفرمود is an honorific past verb meaning "he (politely/royally) said/commanded." تا introduces a subordinate clause meaning "that/so that" (i.e. it introduces what was ordered). سعد is a proper name (Saʿd) in apposition to گوینده, which is a descriptive noun "the speaker/orator." تفت here is the verbal/nominative element meaning "to roast/heat/prepare" (a brief infinitival/nominalized action) that completes the command.
segments.s584-g156: سوی is a directional preposition "toward; to." کلبه is the destination "hut/cottage." مرد is the common noun "man" and نخاس identifies the man as a "peddler/dealer" (appositive), and رفت is the simple past "he went." Grammatically this completes the command clause by showing Saʿd's movement to the peddler's cottage.
segments.s584-g157: بیاورد is a past tense verb meaning "he brought/produced." رومی qualifies the servants as "Roman/Byzantine." کنیزک is a diminutive/noun "young maidservant/handmaid." (The numeral چهل in the line modifies کنیزک but is not listed in the supplied word entries.)
segments.s584-g158: همه is a quantifier "all/everyone." از + در is a poetic locative/partitive phrase "from the door/hall of..." کام means "desire/pleasure" here, آرام qualifies دل as "calm/peaceful," and دل is "heart." Taken together this idiom describes the maidens as coming from a place of pleasure and heart's repose (i.e. they bring delight and calm to the heart).
segments.s584-g159: بگزید is the past tense verb "he chose/selected." بهرام is the proper name "Bahram" and is the subject. زان here means "of those/from those," pointing back to the previously mentioned maidens; گلرخان is an adjective (or descriptive plural) meaning "rose-faced/rosy-cheeked" describing the chosen ones.
segments.s584-g160: که is a relative pronoun "who/whose/that." در + پوستشان is a locative phrase "in/under their skin" (پوستشان = their skin). عاج is a substantive "ivory," and بود is the past copula "was/were." استخوان is the head noun "bones." The relative clause literally says their bones were ivory under their skin (a poetic way to praise their whiteness/fineness).
segments.s584-g121: هم (an adverb) here means “also/too” and is placed at the start to link this line to the previous one. The phrase از داغ دیگر is a prepositional phrase “from another dāgh (wound/brand/mark)”; کمیتی is a diminutive/indefinite noun meaning “a small amount / a little spot” and follows the noun it modifies (داغ). به رنگ is the prepositional unit “in the color of / of the color” that will link to the next phrase describing whose color it resembles.
segments.s584-g122: تو گفتی is a reported-speech clause: تو (you) + گفتی (second-person past tense ‘you said’). Poetic word order places the reported clause before its content. ز دریا برآمد نهنگ is an independent clause: ز = ‘from’, دریا = ‘the sea’, برآمد = past ‘rose/appeared/emerged’, and نهنگ (the subject) follows the verb as is common in older/poetic Persian word order.
segments.s584-g123: همی is an iterative/adverbial particle here meaning ‘still/continually’ or simply giving a habitual/continuous sense before the verb. آتش افروخت is a past simple verbal phrase: آتش (fire, direct object) + افروخت (he kindled/it kindled). از نعل اوی is a prepositional phrase ‘from his horseshoe’: از = from, نعل = horseshoe, اوی = archaic/poetic possessive pronoun ‘his’.
segments.s584-g124: Again همی gives a continuing or vivid action. خون چکید is the verb phrase ‘blood dripped’: خون (blood) + چکید (dripped/fell). از بر لعل اوی is a locative/partitive expression ‘from (upon) the ruby of him’: از = from, بر here carries the sense ‘on/upon’ or ‘off of’, لعل = ruby (a red stone), اوی = his. The clause describes simultaneous vivid imagery.
segments.s584-g125: بها داد is a verb phrase meaning ‘gave the price/paid the value’: بها (price/value) + داد (gave/paid). منذر is the proper name ‘Munḏir’ and is the agent of the verb. چو بود ارزشان is a subordinate clause beginning with چو (= ‘when/as’ here) + بود (past of ‘to be’ = ‘was’) + ارزشان (‘their worth/value’): ‘when/inasmuch as their worth was...’. The clause places the reason or circumstance for the giving.
segments.s584-g126: که introduces a content/explanatory clause (‘that/for’). در بیشه کوفه is a locative phrase ‘in the grove/thicket of Kufa’: در = in, بیشه = grove/thicket, کوفه = Kufa (place). بد مرزشان is a nominal phrase where بد (bad/ill) modifies مرز (border/limit) and شان is the possessive ‘their’: ‘their boundary/stand was poor/unfavourable.’ Together the clause specifies the circumstance tied to their worth.
segments.s584-g127: بپذرفت is a past-tense transitive verb ‘accepted/received’ (literary form). In this poetic order the verb precedes the subject: بهرام (Bahram) is the subject that performs the acceptance. The following ز + و should be read together as زو (= ز + او) meaning ‘from him’ (an older/poetic contraction of از او). آن دو اسپ is the direct object ‘those two horses/steeds’: آن = those/the, دو = two, اسپ = steed/horse.
segments.s584-g128: فروزنده is an adjective/epithet meaning ‘radiant/bright’ placed before the simile. بر سان is a fixed poetic pattern meaning ‘upon/as/like’: بر = on/upon (here connective), سان = like/as. آذر گشسپ is a compound/name (Azar-Goshasp); together the phrase means ‘radiant like Azar-Goshasp’ and modifies the accepted steeds.
segments.s584-g49: تو‌گر is the conditional particle phrase 'تو گر' = 'if you'; باز modifies the verb meaning 'again' or 'once more'; هنگام is the object 'the (right) time/moment' and precedes the verb (normal Persian allows object before verb for emphasis); جویی is the present/imperative-form of the verb 'to seek' addressed to 'you' (classical Persian 2nd‑person present), and همی is the archaic/high-register continuous/aspectual particle placed after the verb to mark repeated/habitual or ongoing action.
segments.s584-g50: دل is the grammatical subject 'heart'; از marks separation/ablative 'of/from' and takes the following plural noun نیکویها 'the good/beautiful things' (here 'beauties' or 'good things'); بشویی is the present (2nd‑person) form 'you wash/cleanse' (ب‑ + شوی) and, together with the sentence's conditional first half, expresses the consequence; همی again is the continuous/aspect particle emphasizing habitual or ongoing action.
segments.s584-g51: همه کار is the subject noun phrase 'every/all work/undertaking'; the two adjectives بی‌گاه و بی‌بر follow the noun (classical Persian permits adjective phrases after the noun) and mean 'untimely and unfruitful/ill‑fated'; بود is the past copula 'was' placed finally as is usual in classical lines.
segments.s584-g52: بهین is a predicative adjective/noun 'the best'; از introduces a genitive/partitive phrase 'of' (here 'of the living body'); تن زندگان is a genitive construction 'the body of the living' (زندگان = 'the living'); سر is 'head' (anatomical/top part) and بود is again the past copula 'was' completing the nominal sentence: 'the best of the living body was the head.'
segments.s584-g53: هران چیز is an older/formal expression 'those things' or 'whatever things'; کان here is a classical relative/linking particle ('which/that') introducing a clause (note: this sense was not listed among the provided senses for کان); در خور is a fixed phrase meaning 'suitable/fitting for' and governs the following noun; پادشاست is a fused form meaning 'is for a king' or 'fit for a king' (پادشاه + است).
segments.s584-g54: بیاموزیم is a hortative/jussive first‑person‑plural 'let us learn' (we should/let us learn); تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that/in order that'; بدانم is the present/subjunctive 1st‑person 'that I may know'; سزاست is a nominal predicate 'it is fitting/merited' placed at the end, so the whole clause reads 'let us learn so that I may know what is fitting.'
segments.s584-g55: سر راستی is a genitive/nominal phrase 'the root/essence of righteousness' (سر used poetically as 'essence' or 'chief'); دانش is the predicate noun 'knowledge'; ایزدیست is the adjectival/copular phrase 'is divine' (ایزدی + است) — together: 'the root of rightness is divine knowledge.'
segments.s584-g56: خنک here is used predicatively/adverbially 'fortunate/blessed' and begins the clause for emphasis; آنک is the relative pronoun 'he who/one who' or 'that which'; بادانش is the fused form of با دانش 'with knowledge' (instrumental/possessive sense); و links and بخردیست is a nominal predicate 'and is of wisdom / is wise' (بخردی = 'with wisdom' plus است). The whole clause means 'blessed is he who has knowledge and wisdom.'
segments.s584-g25: همی is a habitual/continuous adverb placed before the verb to mean 'continually' or 'still'. داشتندش is past tense, 3rd-person plural 'they had/kept' with the enclitic object ‑ش 'him'. چنین چار سال is a duration phrase 'such four years / for four years' (چنین = 'thus/so' modifying the duration), so the whole segment = 'they kept him like that for four years.'
segments.s584-g26: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. شد is the past 'became' (or 'was'). سیرشیر is an archaic/uncertain lexical item here (see note) and so is treated as a lexical uncertainty in this passage. و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. بیاگند is an irregular/archaic verb-form in this context (its exact classical meaning is unclear here) and takes یال ('mane') as its patient, so the segment reads 'when [he] became سیرشیر and [his] mane ...'.
segments.s584-g27: به دشواری is a prepositional phrase 'with difficulty' (به used adverbially). از + شیر + کردن here means 'to take away from the breast / to wean', with کردند as the past plural verb 'they did/caused'. باز is an adverbial particle following the verb (here functioning roughly as an aspectual/continuative or 'away/again' particle in the idiom 'از شیر کردن باز' = 'to wean').
segments.s584-g28: همی again signals continuous action. داشتندش is past plural 'they kept him' with the object clitic ‑ش. The sequence به بر بر is a repetitive, idiomatic placement of prepositions/particles (بر = 'on/upon') giving a sense of holding on the arm or in the lap; به ناز = 'with tenderness/affection' (به + ناز). Altogether: 'they kept him on their arms, with pampering.'
segments.s584-g29: چو شد هفت ساله is a temporal clause 'when he became seven years old' (چو = 'when', شد = past 'became', هفت‌ساله = 'seven‑year‑old'). به منذر is the prepositional phrase 'to Mundhir' (addressing the named person). چه گفت is an interrogative 'what did he say' with چه as the question word and گفت as the past-tense verb 'said' (here the verb introduces the reported speech that follows).
segments.s584-g30: که introduces the content clause ('that ...'). آن رای = 'that counsel/opinion' (آن = 'that', رای = 'counsel/plan'). با مهتری means 'with the elder/chief' (با = 'with', مهتری = 'the elder/chief'); بود is the past copula 'was'. جفت here means 'fit' or 'matching' (literally 'pair'), so the clause = 'that counsel was a match/suited the elder.'
segments.s584-g31: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said' in past). کای is the vocative particle 'O' addressing someone directly; مهتر سرفراز is the vocative noun phrase 'exalted/illustrious elder' (مهتر = 'chief/elder', سرفراز = 'proud/exalted'). The phrase is the speaker's direct address following the reporting verb.
segments.s584-g32: ز من is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from me' or 'because of me' (ز = 'from'). کودک شیرخواره is a nominal phrase 'a suckling/ nursing child' (کودک = 'child', شیرخواره = 'suckling/at-the-breast'). مساز is a negative imperative 'do not make' (مساز = negative of ساز 'make' — classical negative m‑ prefix on the imperative), so the clause is an appeal: 'do not make (me) a suckling child.'
segments.s584-g57: نگه کرد is the verbal phrase from نگه‌کردن (to look/watch) in the simple past (he looked). منذر is the person-name placed after the verb (common in poetic word order) and بدو is the dative pronoun 'to him/at him'; خیره ماند is a predicate: خیره (staring/astounded) + ماند (remained) = 'remained staring'. Together: 'Munḏir looked (at him) and remained staring.'
segments.s584-g58: به زیر لبان is an adverbial idiom 'under the lips' = 'under his breath / in a low voice' (به here functions adverbially). نام یزدان بخواند is a past-tense utterance: نام یزدان (the name of God) + بخواند (classical poetic past/preterite 'he uttered/recited'); thus 'he uttered/muttered the name of God under his breath.'
segments.s584-g59: فرستاد is the simple past 'he sent.' هم adds 'also/likewise.' در زمان is the temporal prepositional phrase 'at the time/at the moment of' and رهنمون ('guide' / 'appointed guidance') here describes that time or instruction — 'at the appointed time' or 'at the time set by the guide/instruction.'
segments.s584-g60: سوی marks direction 'toward.' شورستان is the place-name/description 'the scene/place of tumult or revelry.' سرکشی here denotes 'rebellion/insubordination' (the thing to be handled) and بر + هیون = 'against the beast' (بر as 'against/over' and هیون 'beast/monster'). The whole phrase expresses sending someone toward the uproar to confront the beast's rebellion.
segments.s584-g61: سه موبد is a numeral + noun 'three mobeds (priests).' نگه کرد is the past 'kept watch/looked.' فرهنگ جوی is a compound: فرهنگ 'learning/culture' + جوی (from جوینده 'seeker') = 'seekers of learning,' functioning as a modifier describing those mobeds.
segments.s584-g62: که is a relative pronoun 'who/which.' در شورستان 'in the place of revelry' gives the location. بودشان is composed of بود (was) + شان (their/for them) and means 'they had' or 'was to them' — here 'they had (a) ...' — and آب‌روی (lit. 'water-face') is an idiom meaning 'reputation/standing.' So: 'who had reputation in the revelrous place.'
segments.s584-g63: یکی here is 'one/a certain one' (a single person among them). تا introduces purpose 'so that / in order that,' and دبیری (the noun from دبیری) means 'secretarial/secretaryly art, chancery skill' (teaching administrative or literary skills). بیاموزدش is a subjunctive/optative form (بـ + آموزد) meaning 'may he teach him' or 'to teach him' with the enclitic -ش referring to 'him.' The structure expresses purpose: one (to be) so that he teach him the art of chancery.
segments.s584-g64: دل is the direct object 'the heart.' از تیرگیها is an ablative phrase 'from the darknesses' (i.e., from ignorance/gloom). بیفروزدش (بی-فروزد + ش) is a desiderative/optative poetic form 'may he illumine/brighten him,' with -ش again referring to 'him.' Together: '(so that) he may teach him chancery and brighten his heart, freeing it from darkness.'
segments.s584-g161: به بالا is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'upwards/high above' (به + بالا). The second به + کردار marks manner: به کردار X = 'in the manner/likeness of X'. سربو/سرو سهی is a noun + epithet: سرو (cypress) + سهی (an epithet meaning dark) forming the object of 'کردار'. Word order is poetic: adverbial (به بالا) + manner phrase (به کردار ...) with an omitted copula.
segments.s584-g162: همه introduces a three‑part list 'all/every'. The items are nouns: کام (fulfillment/satisfaction), زیبای (here 'beauty' — note the form is split in the edition), and فرهی (nobility/worthiness). The final short element ی corresponds to an archaic/poetic existence/copula ('is' / 'there is') that links the list to the preceding description; Persian poetry frequently omits an explicit verb and uses such brief forms.
segments.s584-g163: ازان دو ستاره is a genitive/partitive phrase 'of those two stars'. یکی functions as the predicate 'one (of them)' and is immediately followed by an apposition noun چنگ‌زن 'harp‑player'. The copula 'was' is omitted (typical in classical Persian), so the phrase reads 'one (was) a harp‑player.'
segments.s584-g164: دگر 'the other' begins the second member of the contrast. لاله رخ is a compound attributive (literally 'tulip‑faced', i.e. a rosy/flowerlike face). چون introduces a simile 'like/as', and سهیل یمن names the star (Suhail/Canopus) associated with Yemen — a poetic locution. Again the copula is omitted: 'the other (was) tulip‑faced like Suhail of Yemen.'
segments.s584-g165: به بالا چون سرو repeats the adverbial 'high above' (به بالا) and a simile 'چون سرو' ('like a cypress'). The conjunction و links a second descriptive phrase: به گیسو کمند — 'with (her) hair [as] a snare' (به marking the instrumental/associative relation, and the noun کمند 'snare' predicated of گیسو 'tresses'). The construction omits a copular verb; both phrases are descriptive qualifiers.
segments.s584-g166: بها داد is a verb phrase 'gave (a) price/value/honor' where بها is the object (here 'honour/gift') and داد is the past tense 'gave'. The proper name منذر follows the verb (V–S order common in classical Persian). چو آمد is a temporal subordinate 'when he came' (چو = 'when'), and پسند is a noun 'approval/liking' implying 'it pleased (him)' — the clause thus says 'Munḏir gave the gift when he arrived, (to his) liking/approval.'
segments.s584-g167: بخندید بهرام و کرد آفرین is two coordinated past actions. بخندید is a perfective past (3rd person) with verb-before-subject order (poetic inversion) meaning “laughed (Bahram).” و links the clauses (“and”), and کرد is the past of کردن used as a light verb meaning “made/uttered” together with the noun آفرین; آفرین here is an interjection (‘bravo’/praise) rather than the verbal root “to create.”
segments.s584-g168: رخش گشت همچون بدخشان نگین: رخش is the subject (a poetic word for steed/horse), گشت is past of گشتن used causatively/cessative “became/turned into” (change of state), and همچون introduces a simile “like.” بدخشان نگین is a genitive/attributive phrase “the gem of Badakhshan” (region name modifying نگین), so the whole clause says the steed became like a Badakhshan gem. Word order is noun + verb + simile phrase, a typical concise poetic structure.
segments.s584-g81: This is a compact predicative clause: the preposition به + موبد marks the beneficiary/experiencer ('for the mobad'), followed by نبودش (past negative with the enclitic -ش referring to 'him') meaning 'he did not have' / 'he needed not'. The phrase به چیزی نیاز places the nominal object of lack after نبودش in Persian order: 'to/for anything need' → 'had no need of anything'.
segments.s584-g82: This continues the complement of 'نبودش به چیزی نیاز' and specifies sources/items he did not need. The initial به again marks 'from/for', followed by a compound noun 'فرهنگ جویان' ('seekers of learning/culture'). The coordination 'و آن یوز و باز' lists two animals; آن is a demonstrative 'that', and the sequence 'یوز و باز' is a simple 'X and Y' list functioning as further objects of 'نیاز نداشت'.
segments.s584-g83: A sequence of locative/directional phrase + infinitival verb: 'به آوردگه' = 'to/at the gathering-place'. 'بر عنان تافتن' is an older/poetic infinitive construction where بر+عنان = 'upon the reins' and تافتن (here used poetically) means 'to glint/brandish' — i.e. to flourish or make the reins show. The clause is an action described by an unconjugated verb, with the subject omitted (understood from context: they).
segments.s584-g84: Two coordinated infinitival actions joined by 'و هم' (and also): 'برافگندن اسپ' is a transitive infinitive phrase 'to fling/throw (onto) the horse' (here idiomatically 'to vault onto the horse'); 'هم تاختن' adds 'and also to gallop/charge'. Both verbs are in the non-finite form and share the same omitted plural subject 'they'.
segments.s584-g85: This is a reporting clause: 'به منذر چنین گفت' = 'to Munḏir he said thus' (به + name marks the addressee). What follows is direct speech introduced without a separate quotation marker. 'کای' is a vocative particle 'O!', and 'پاک‌رای' is an epithet 'pure-minded' or 'of pure reputation' addressing the addressee.
segments.s584-g86: This is the imperative content of the speech. 'گسی' = 'someone' (indefinite person) followed by the imperative 'کن' = 'do/send (let there be)'. The object 'هنرمند را' is marked with the direct-object particle را, and 'باز جای' is an adjectival/adverbial phrase meaning 'again to his place' or 'restore to his post'. Together: 'Send someone; restore the craftsman to his place.'
segments.s584-g87: 'ازان' ('from/of that/those') introduces what follows; 'هر یکی را' ('each one' with object marker را) marks the recipients. 'بسی هدیه' places the quantifier/adverb 'بسی' ('many/enough/plentiful') before the noun 'هدیه' and 'داد' is the simple past 'gave'. The whole clause means 'he gave each of them ample gifts.'
segments.s584-g88: 'ز درگاه منذر' is a poetic prepositional phrase ('from the court of Munḏir') where ز = 'from'. 'برفتند' is the past plural 'they departed/went off' and 'شاد' is a predicative/adverbial adjective 'glad/joyful', so the clause reads 'they left Munḏir's court joyful.'
segments.s584-g9: ز is the poetic form of از, a preposition meaning 'from'; it begins a range that is completed by تا. ایوان (iwan/porch) is the head noun, followed by the title-phrase شاه جهان ('king of the world') in postnominal apposition, so the whole is 'the iwan of the king of the world'. تا به دشت marks the limit 'as far as/to the plain' (تا + به used to indicate the spatial endpoint).
segments.s584-g10: همی is the archaic/epic continuous particle often prefixed to verbs to give a sustained or iterative sense ('kept on / proceeded'); the verb گذشت (past 'passed') occurs sentence-final as normal in Persian. The coordination اشتر و اسپ و هودج lists the things that passed before the verb (noun … و … و … + verb).
segments.s584-g11: پرستنده + و + دایه is a coordinated noun phrase 'attendants and nurses'. بی‌شمار is a postnominal adjective meaning 'countless/numerous' modifying the compound. (In Classical/Ferdowsi style noun pairs like this often denote both professional attendants and wet-nurses accompanying a courtly procession.)
segments.s584-g12: ز ... تا introduces a spatial range 'from X to Y': ز بازارگه 'from the marketplace' and تا در شهریار 'up to the king's door/court'. Here در is used in its secondary sense 'door/gate' (درِ شهریار = the king's gate/threshold), so the phrase indicates extent of the crowd.
segments.s584-g13: This is an absolute/participial-like clause: بازار گه بسته آیین = 'the marketplace/bazaars being closed in ceremony' where بسته is a past participle 'closed' modifying بازارگه and آیین specifies that the closure was ceremonial. به راه is a prepositional/directional phrase 'on the road; they took to the road' introducing the movement.
segments.s584-g14: Again a range: ز دروازه 'from the gate' تا 'as far as' پیش درگاه شاه 'before / in the presence of the king's court'. پیش here means 'in front of / before (in the presence of)', and درگاه carries the sense 'royal court / throne-room', so the whole phrase indicates the procession's route ending at the king's presence.
segments.s584-g15: منذر is the personal name 'Munḏir' (subject); بیامد is the past tense 'came' (bi‑ prefix + آمد, a common classical/past form). به شهر یمن is the directional phrase 'to the city of Yemen'. The preceding جو is anomalous here as a standalone item in the wordlist (it is not functioning as the common noun 'barley' in context), so its sense is unclear in this line.
segments.s584-g16: پذیره is a noun 'reception/welcome' placed before the copular/predicativeشدندش. شدند is the third-person plural past 'they became' and the enclitic ش = 'him' (direct object) attaches to it; together پذیره شدندش yields the idiomatic 'they received/welcomed him'. همه مرد و زن is a fixed expression 'all men and women' = 'everyone.'
segments.s584-g137: Word order is poetic: ز + noun (ز اندوه) = 'from/because of sorrow'; باشد is the copular/auxiliary here meaning 'is / becomes' (present/habitual sense); رخ مرد زرد = 'the man's face (رخِ مرد) [is] pale (زرد)'. In short: 'From sorrow the man's face is pale.'
segments.s584-g138: به رامش is instrumental/benefactive ('with/through tranquility/solace'); فزاید is 3rd-person singular present 'increases / brings about'; تن زادمرد = 'the body of the zād‑mard (noble‑born man)'. The clause means that tranquility/solace increases (i.e., brings) calm to the nobleman's body.
segments.s584-g139: برین‌بر here functions idiomatically as 'besides this / upon this'; یکی خوبی افزای is an exhortation 'add one goodness / do one good deed' with افزای as an imperative/jussive 'add'. پس links to the following explanatory clause ('for/so').
segments.s584-g140: که heads an explanatory clause meaning 'for/since'; باشد is the copula 'is' (here giving a general statement); ز هر درد = 'from every pain/affliction' (ز = 'from'); فریادرس is a substantive 'rescuer, succorer'. Together: 'for he is a rescuer from every affliction.'
segments.s584-g141: This is a paired conditional: اگر ... اگر ... = 'if/whether ... or ...'. تاجدارست = 'is crowned' (tāj-dār + ast contracted), پهلوان = 'a champion/hero'. The sequence lists alternative statuses of the person.
segments.s584-g142: به زن گیرد literally 'takes to a woman/wife' (به = 'to', زن = 'woman/wife', گیرد = 'he takes'); آرام modifies the result 'is calmed/settled'; مرد جوان is the subject/patient 'the young man'. The clause says that upon marrying/taking a wife the young man becomes calm.
segments.s584-g143: همان 'that/for that reason' introduces the result; ز + (او) is an archaic contraction (زو) meaning 'from him / because of him' — here treated as 'from that'; بود is the past copula 'was'; دین یزدان = 'the religion of God' and به پای = 'at the feet of' (a formulaic locative: 'placed at the feet of'). The clause overall: 'For that reason/from him, God's law was at the (the) feet (i.e. before) ...'.
segments.s584-g144: جوان را uses را as the object/target marker ('to/for the young'); به نیکی = 'in goodness / by goodness' (به + noun gives an adverbial/instrumental sense); بود رهنمای = 'was a guide'. Put together: 'He was a guide to the young in goodness.'
segments.s584-g65: دگر (an index word meaning 'another/other') begins a new item in a list. آنک is a relative pronoun 'who/that' introducing the clause. دانستن here is a verbal noun/infinitive 'the knowing/knowledge (how to handle)' and takes the following objects باز و یوز ('hawk and cheetah') as the skills named. Word order is typical older Persian: determiner/relative + verbal-noun + objects.
segments.s584-g66: بیاموزدش is a 3rd-person singular teaching-verb with the enclitic object -ش ('him/it'); in classical style it functions as 'taught him' (the narrative past sense). کان بود is a short copular phrase: کان functions as a demonstrative/identifying particle ('that one/it was') and بود is the past copula 'was'. دلفروز is the predicate proper name identifying the person.
segments.s584-g67: ودیگر ('and another/and others') continues the list; که is the relative pronoun 'who/which' introducing what this other taught. چوگان, تیر, کمان are coordinate objects ('polo / the mallet or game', 'arrow', 'bow') listed in the natural order noun-and-noun with و 'and' linking them.
segments.s584-g68: همان is a demonstrative 'the same' pointing back to a skill or pattern already mentioned; گردش here means 'turn/maneuver' or 'movement' and is linked to رزم 'of battle/war' (i.e. 'maneuvers of war'). با introduces an adverbial phrase 'with ...' and بدگمان is an adverb/adjective meaning 'suspiciously/with distrust' describing the manner. The clause lacks an explicit finite verb because it continues the relative-description construction.
segments.s584-g69: چپ و راست are directional adverbs 'left and right' presented in the common paired order. پیچان is a participial/adjectival form 'twisting' modifying عنان 'the reins' and عنان داشتن is the light-verb construction 'to hold/have the reins' (داشتن as the main verb). The phrase describes continuous or habitual action (typical in narrative lists of skills).
segments.s584-g70: به is the directional preposition 'to/at/into'. آوردگه is a locative noun 'place of arrival / the field/arena' and stands as the place where the action occurs. باره here is an object meaning 'load/occasion' (something to be lifted or carried) and برگاشتن is the verb 'to lift/take up' (بر- + گاشتن), forming the action performed at the arena.
segments.s584-g71: چنین 'thus/so' sets the manner. موبدان is the plural of موبد 'mōbed' (Zoroastrian priest/learned man). پیش + منذر شدند is a fixed locution meaning 'they came/appeared before Munḏir' or 'they presented themselves to Munḏir' — پیش here 'in the presence of' and شدند the 3rd-person plural past 'they became/were/appeared'.
segments.s584-g72: ز is the classical preposition 'from'. هر دانشی 'every/each kind of knowledge' uses دانشی as a genitive noun ('of knowledge'). داستانها زدند uses زدند in the performative sense 'they struck/sounded/recited' — here idiomatically 'they related/told tales' (i.e. they recited stories drawn from every branch of learning).
segments.s584-g41: چنین modifies the whole clause ('thus/so'); پاسخ آورد is a verbal phrase where آورد is the simple past 3rd-person of آوردن ('gave/uttered') and پاسخ is its object ('gave an answer'). بهرام is the subject (proper name) and باز follows as an adverb meaning 'again' — classical word order is flexible, so subject appears between verb and object here.
segments.s584-g42: که is a complementizer introducing reported speech or content ('that'); از من marks the cause/agent ('by me' / 'because of me'); تو is the 2nd-person pronoun. بی‌کار (adjective) + خوردی uses خوردی in the intransitive sense 'befell/wasfall upon' so 'you became idle' (past); مساز is an imperative with negative particle built in (from ساختن) meaning 'do not make/contrive' — here 'do not claim/pretend'.
segments.s584-g43: مرا هست دانـش is an existential/possessive construction: 'to me there is knowledge' = 'I have knowledge' (مرا as dative/topic, هست copula/existential). اگر سال نیست is a concessive clause 'if/though there is no years/age' (سال here = 'age/years', نیست = 'is not').
segments.s584-g44: بسان is a preposition/particle 'like/as'; گوانم here means 'I am like (the) gawān' or 'like them' (classical simile). بر و یال نیست is a nominal phrase where بر (here spatial/part reference) and یال ('mane') are negated by نیست: 'I am not without (back/strength) and mane' — i.e. not lacking those attributes. Word order places the simile phrase before the negated nouns.
segments.s584-g45: ترا is here the clitic combination of 2nd-person suffix + object marker (rendered in the data as ت + را) and with هست yields 'you have' or 'there is to you' ('you have years'). و links clauses; خرد کمترست is a predicative phrase with کمترست (comparative + copula fused) 'is less' — 'your wisdom is less.'
segments.s584-g46: نهاد (noun) = 'disposition/nature'; من is the possessor 'my'. از رای تو is a comparative/origin phrase 'from your opinion/counsel' (از = 'from/of'); دیگرست is the copula + adjective 'is different.' The whole clause states that the speaker's nature differs from the addressee's counsel.
segments.s584-g47: ندانی is the negative present 2nd-person form ('you do not know') built from دانستن; the first که introduces the content clause. هرکس که is a free relative 'whoever/anyone who'; هنگام جست combines هنگام 'at the time (of)' with جست — here the past/stative sense 'sought' or 'seeks' (context: 'when he seeks').
segments.s584-g48: ز (poetic form of از) is the preposition 'from'; کار آن = 'that work/affair'; گزیند is 3rd-person present/past (classical) of گزیندن 'chooses/selects' (here habitual/general present). The relative clause که باید نخست modifies what is chosen: 'that which ought to be first' (باید = 'must/ought', نخست = 'first').
segments.s584-g17: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. آمد is past-tense 3rd-person singular 'came'. به آرامگاه is a prepositional phrase 'to the tomb/resting‑place' (به + noun). از نخست is an adverbial phrase 'at first/initially' placed after the location; the whole segment means 'when he came to the tomb, at first...'.
segments.s584-g18: فراوان here modifies the noun phrase and means 'many/abundant'. زنان نژادی is a noun + postpositive qualifier meaning 'women of lineage / of noble stock' (نژادی = 'of descent'). بجست is a past action 'sprang/leapt forward' (classical epic usage where the verb follows the descriptive phrase); together: 'many women of noble birth sprang forward.'
segments.s584-g19: ز is the poetic/prepositional 'from/among'. The list 'دهقان و تازی و پرمایگان' names social types: دهقان 'peasant / dehqān', تازی 'Arab', پرمایگان 'the great ones / nobles'. The conjunction و simply links the items.
segments.s584-g20: This is a nominal phrase describing the people chosen: توانگر 'wealthy', گزیده 'selected/choice', گران 'precious/valued' modifying the head noun سایگان. سایگان here means 'retainers/protectors/attendants' (not a physical shade). The verb of selection is implied from the neighboring lines (the clause continues the idea of choosing).
segments.s584-g21: ازین = از + این 'from these'. مهتران (chiefs/elders/notables) is the plural noun meaning important men; چار 'four' is the numeral and زن 'women' is the noun object. برگزید is the past-tense verb 'chose/selected'. The literal order is 'from these chiefs four women he chose.'
segments.s584-g22: که here introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that/that'. آید is present/subjunctive-like 'may come' or 'comes'. هنر means 'excellence/virtue/skill' and بر is the preposition 'upon/on'. نژادش = نژاد + -ش 'his lineage'. پدید (پدید آمدن) means 'to appear/manifests' and here follows to complete the verbal idea: 'that excellence may appear upon his lineage.'
segments.s584-g23: دو before nouns is the numeral 'two', so 'دو تازی دو دهقان' = 'two Arabs and two peasants'. ز تخم کیان is a genitival/origin phrase 'of the stock/seed of the Kians' (ز + تخم = 'from the lineage of'), attributing their origin to the Kian royal line.
segments.s584-g24: ببستند is the past 3rd‑plural form 'they bound/arranged/concluded' (here 'they assigned/arranged'). مرا (presented split in the word list) is the object 'me' (accusative). دایگی را is the definite object 'the wet‑nursing' (دایگی = wet‑nursing) with را marking it, and میان 'between' indicates distribution among them: 'they arranged the wet‑nursing of me between (them).' The verb precedes its objects in this classical order.
segments.s584-g1: چو بشنید = 'when he heard' — چو is the temporal conjunction 'when', followed by بشنید (past 3rd sg. 'he heard'). ز + و here mark the source cliticually ('from him'); the phrase این سخن یزدگرد follows as the content heard. Word order is poetic (verb before some modifiers), so literally 'When he heard from him this speech, Yazdegerd...'.
segments.s584-g2: روان و خرد را برآورد گرد = 'he rallied/raised his spirit and reason' — روان و خرد را is the definite object phrase (را marks it). برآورد is a past action 'brought up / restored' and گرد here is an archaic/poetic element linked to the verbal phrase; together they mean he recovered or roused his inner composure and wisdom.
segments.s584-g3: نگه کرد = 'he looked/kept his gaze' — نگه (gaze/watch) + کرد (past auxiliary 'did' making the compound verb). از آغاز فرجام را literally 'from the beginning (to) the end' — فرجام را is a definite object marked by را; the whole means he surveyed/observed from start to finish.
segments.s584-g4: بدو داد پرمایه بهرام را = 'he gave him (بدو) — he gave (داد) — the substantial/valuable thing (پرمایه) — Bahram (بهرام) marked as definite by را.' In this order, بدو marks the indirect recipient 'to him', while بهرام را is the definite direct object; overall meaning: he handed over Bahram's valuable gift (or gave Bahram a valuable gift), the poetic word order placing recipients and objects before the verb.
segments.s584-g5: بفرمود تا خلعتش ساختند = 'He commanded (بفرمود) that (تا introduces the resultant clause) they make his robe.' بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb 'he said/commanded', تا introduces the subordinate result/purpose clause, خلعتش is 'his robe' (خلعت + possessive), and ساختند is the past plural 'they made'.
segments.s584-g6: سرش را به گردون برافراختند = 'they raised his head to the sky/heavens.' سرش را is the object ('his head' + definite marker را), به گردون is a prepositional phrase 'toward the sky/heaven', and برافراختند is the past plural verb 'they lifted/raised up', a common poetic expression meaning to exalt or display someone publicly.
segments.s584-g7: تنش را به خلعت بیاراستند = 'They adorned his body with the robe.' تنش را (his body + definite marker را) is the object, به خلعت 'with a robe' indicates the means or ornament, and بیاراستند is the past plural 'they adorned/arrayed', a transitive perfective verb.
segments.s584-g8: ز در اسپ شاه یمن خواستند = 'From the gate (ز در) they requested/asked for (خواستند) the steed (اسپ) of the king of Yemen (شاه یمن).' The genitive relation is expressed by juxtaposing اسپ (steed) with شاه یمن (the king of Yemen), and خواستند is past plural 'they requested/wished for.'
segments.s584-g97: گله‌دار اسپان من پیش تست = 'the herdsman of my horses is before you.' گله‌دار اسپان من is a noun phrase 'the herdsman of my horses' (گله‌دار = herdsman, اسپان = horses, من = my/I used possessively). پیش تست is an archaic/poetic compression of پیشت است ('is before you') where پیشت = 'in front of you' and the copula است is fused; syntactically this places the existence/location predicate after the possessor phrase.
segments.s584-g98: خداوند او هم به تن خویش تست = 'his lord/master also is himself present in person.' خداوند او هم 'his lord too' (خداوند here = master/owner, او = he/his, هم = also). به تن خویش = 'in his own person' (به + تن 'body/person' + خویش/ش = possessive 'his own'), and تست is again the poetic/copular form corresponding to 'is' (establishing presence).
segments.s584-g99: گر از تازیان اسپ خواهی خرید = 'If (گر) you want (خواهی) to buy (خرید) horses (اسپ) from the Arabs (از تازیان).' گر introduces a conditional; از تازیان is a prepositional phrase ('from the Arabs' or 'of the Arabs'); خواهی + خرید is a desiderative/future-like construction where خواهی governs the action 'خرید' (to buy).
segments.s584-g100: مرا رنج و سختی چه باید کشید = literally 'What suffering and hardship must be suffered by me?' مرا is the accusative/affected pronoun 'me' (here marking the experiencer), رنج و سختی are the coordinated objects 'suffering and hardship', چه is the interrogative 'what/how much', and باید کشید is an impersonal/obligative construction 'must be endured' (باید = 'it is necessary', کشید here = 'to suffer/undergo'); word order places the experiencer 'مرا' before the patient nouns for emphasis.
segments.s584-g101: بدو گفت بهرام کای نیک‌نام = 'Bahram said to him, "O well‑reputed one."' بدو is the dative/prepositional 'to him', گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (word order here places the indirect object/dative before the verb), بهرام names the speaker, and کای نیک‌نام is a vocative 'O (kay) well‑reputed one' (کای = 'O', a poetic vocative; نیک‌نام = 'well‑reputed').
segments.s584-g102: به نیکیت بادا همه‌ساله کام = a short optative blessing: به نیکیت 'for your goodness/for your welfare' (به = 'to/for', نیکیت = 'your goodness' formed from نیک), بادا is an optative particle 'may it be', and همه‌ساله کام 'every year (همه‌ساله) [may there be] fulfillment/wish (کام)'. The clause is elliptical: 'May every year bring the fulfillment of your goodness.'
segments.s584-g103: من اسپ آن گزینم که اندر نشیب = 'I choose that horse which on the descent ...' من is the first‑person subject 'I', اسپ آن گزینم is 'I choose that steed' (آن = 'that', گزینم = 1st person present/optative of 'choose'), and که اندر نشیب introduces the relative clause 'which, in/at the descent' (اندر = in/within, نشیب = descent/slope).
segments.s584-g104: بتازم نه بینم عنان از رکیب = 'I gallop (بتازم) so that I do not see the reins from the rider.' بتازم is a 1st‑person present/future-like verb 'I gallop/rush' (poetic form), نه بینم is the negative + verb 'I do not see' (نه negates the verb بینم), and عنان از رکیب is the object phrase 'the reins from the rider' (عنان = reins/bridle, از = from/of, رکیب = the rider/companion); together this describes riding so fast that the rider's reins are not seen/held.
segments.s584-g73: تن شاه زاده بدیشان سپرد is a simple past clause: سپرد is the past-tense verb 'entrusted/handed over' with بدیشان 'to them' as its indirect object. تنِ شاه‌زاده (literally 'the body/person of the prince') is the direct object placed before the verb, a common word order in older Persian.
segments.s584-g74: فزاینده خود دانشی بود و گرد is a predicative clause with بود (past 'was') as the copula. فزاینده modifies the following verbless complement to mean 'increasing'; خود here emphasizes the subject ('he himself'). دانشی is an abstract noun 'knowledge' in the oblique form, and the coordinating و links دانشی بود to گرد, a short poetic word that here functions as a second predicate meaning something like 'and widespread/renowned' (the exact nuance is poetic).
segments.s584-g75: چنان گشت is an adverbial-result clause: چنان 'thus/so' + گشت 'became'. The proper name بهرام is the subject and خسرونژاد is an epithet/postmodifier ('of Khosrow's line'), placed after the name as is common in Persian epithets.
segments.s584-g76: که introduces a relative/result clause ('so that/so as to'). اندر هنر = 'in/with regard to art or skill' (اندر = in, هنر = skill). داد here is the verb 'gave' used idiomatically in a phrase with مردی; the sequence مردی بداد is an older idiom meaning he showed or bestowed manly prowess — i.e., he surpassed or proved himself a man in skill. The construction uses داد as a light verb in an idiomatic expression rather than literal 'to hand over'.
segments.s584-g77: هنر هرچ بگذشت بر گوش اوی is a relative/nominal clause: هنر 'art/skill' modified by هرچ 'whatever/whatever sort', then the past verb بگذشت 'passed/was heard' governs the prepositional phrase بر گوش اوی 'upon his ear' (اوی is the older possessive form 'his'). Word order places the object phrase before the verb, typical in classical Persian.
segments.s584-g78: به فرهنگ یازان شدی هوش اوی is a result clause: به فرهنگ 'to/for learning or culture' + یازان (a predicative adjective 'open') + شدی (past 'became') yields 'became open to learning'. هوش اوی 'his intellect/mind' is the subject phrase placed at the end; اوی again is the possessive 'his'.
segments.s584-g79: چو شد سال آن نامور is a temporal clause: چو 'when' + شد 'became/arrived' with سال آن نامور 'that celebrated year' as the temporal noun phrase. The following بر سه شش uses بر with numerals (سه شش = 'three or six', an idiomatic small-number expression) to indicate the group/number among whom he excelled.
segments.s584-g80: دلاور گوی گشت خورشیدفش: گشت is past 'became' and takes the predicate before it. دلاور گوی functions as a descriptive compound ('among/for heroes' or 'called a valiant one' —  گوی here has a performative/nominal sense), so the clause says he became (was) a conspicuous hero; خورشیدفش is an epithet/name applied to him (literally 'sun‑bright' or a poetic epithet).
segments.s584-g145: کنیزک is the addressee/recipient noun 'the little maid'. بفرمای is a polite/imperative marker (from فرمود/فرما) meaning 'say/command' here: 'he said/ordered'. تا پنج و شش is an idiomatic numeral phrase: تا + numeral range means 'about/approximately five or six' (تا used here as 'as far as/even'); the conjunction و simply links the two numerals.
segments.s584-g146: بیارند is a jussive/subjunctive 3rd‑person plural form meaning 'let them bring' (poetic contraction of بیاورند); it depends on the preceding command. با = 'with'. زیب and خورشیدفش are names/epithets presented as companions: زیب functions as a name derived from 'beauty', و is 'and', and خورشیدفش is an epithet/name 'Khurshidfash' (lit. 'sun‑spreading' or similar).
segments.s584-g147: مگر works here as a conditional 'unless' (introducing the alternative). زان = 'from that / from them'. یکی دو = 'one or two' (a small number). گزین is the verb 'to choose/select' (infinitival/stem form), and آیدم is the 1st‑person present 'I come' used here with a future/intent sense ('I will come' or 'I shall come') — the verb order is poetic (verb before subject).
segments.s584-g148: هم = 'also'. اندیشه here means 'intention/mental disposition' (i.e., a settled thought or purpose). آفرین in this slot functions as 'praiseworthy'/'with praise' or as a nominal 'praise' (poetic usage), so اندیشه آفرین = 'a praiseworthy/hopeful intention' (or 'with praise in mind'). آیدم again 'I come' (first person, used optatively/future‑oriented: 'I will come').
segments.s584-g149: مگر here has the sense 'perhaps/otherwise' (introducing a tentative alternative). نیز = 'also'. فرزند = 'child/offspring'. بینم is 1st‑person present/subjunctive 'may I see / I might see' (expressing a wish or possibility). یکی = 'one' (a single child).
segments.s584-g150: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. آرام دل = a phrase meaning 'calming to the heart' (آرام modifies state, دل = 'heart'). باشدم is an archaic/poetic optative/subjunctive form ('may he be / might it be') used here with the sense 'who might be' or 'who would be' — note the -م ending is the old subjunctive/optative marker. اندکی = 'a little' (i.e., a small amount of comfort).
segments.s584-g151: جهاندار = 'the sovereign' or 'world‑lord' (the ruler). خشنود = 'pleased' (predicate adjective). باشد is a subjunctive/jussive form here meaning 'may he be' or 'let him be' (a wish). ز من = 'with me / because of me' (z used poetically = 'from, because of, with').
segments.s584-g152: ستوده is the past‑participle/adjectival 'praised' (predicate). بمانم is 1st‑person subjunctive/optative 'may I remain / let me remain' — together ستوده بمانم = 'may I remain praised'. به هر انجمن = 'in/at every assembly/gathering' (به + هر + انجمن: location/setting 'in every meeting').
segments.s584-g89: This is a poetic, slightly inverted reporting clause. وزان پس (literally “and/from that, then”) places the action after the previous event; به منذر marks the indirect object with به + name (“to Munḏir”). چنین گفت شاه places the verb گفت before the subject شاه (common inversion in poetry) and means “thus said the king.”
segments.s584-g90: که introduces a reported/content clause (’that/—:’). اسپان این نیزه‌داران is an older-style possession/juxtaposition meaning “the horses of these spear-bearers” (اسپان = object, followed by its modifier); بخواه is a hortative/imperative/subjunctive form of خواستن addressed to Munḏir: ‘ask for/claim’.
segments.s584-g91: بگو is an imperative ‘tell (them)’. تا here introduces purpose/result (‘so that/in order that’) and governs the following verb. بپیچند is a 3rd-person plural present-subjunctive/imperative-like form (the subjunctive after تا for purpose: ‘that they may turn’). پیشم عنان is a prepositional/locative phrase ‘before me, the reins’—i.e. guiding their horses in front of the speaker.
segments.s584-g92: به introduces the target of sight (‘to/into’). چشم اندر آرند is a poetic construction: اندر + verb means ‘into’/‘within’, and آرند (3rd‑pl. subjunctive/present) = ‘bring/put (into view)’. نوک سنان is the object ‘the tips of the spears’; word order places the thing brought (نوک سنان) after the verb phrase.
segments.s584-g93: بهایی کنند is a causative/let-structure meaning ‘let them give/set a value’ (بهایی = value/price). آنچ is a relative/indefinite ‘that which/whatever’, and آید خوشم is an idiomatic expression ‘comes pleasing to me’ = ‘that pleases me’ (آید = ‘comes/happens’; خوشم = ‘it pleases me’).
segments.s584-g94: درم is the object (dirhams/coins). پیش is adverbial ‘before/in the presence (of them or the addressee)’. خواهم is the first‑person future/auxiliary ‘I will’. بریشان is the contracted prepositional cluster بر ایشان = ‘to/for them’. کشم is the verb ‘I will draw/pull out’ used idiomatically for ‘I will pay/produce (the money)’. The clause therefore states the speaker’s intention to pay the coins up front.
segments.s584-g95: چنین پاسخ آورد is a standard narrative line ‘thus (چنین) an answer (پاسخ) he brought/gave (آورد)’. The verb precedes the subject in this poetic word order (آورد منذر). بدوی is an adverb here meaning ‘straightaway/at once’ (poetic; an older form), so the whole phrase means ‘Munḏir replied at once thus.’
segments.s584-g96: This is a direct address introduced abruptly (که‌ای = که + ای, a vocative-like particle). پر هنر is an epithet ‘full of art/skill’ (پر = full (of) + هنر = skill). خسرو is the name being addressed, and نامجوی is an epithet ‘one who seeks a name/fame’ placed after the name as a descriptive tag.
segments.s584-g33: به introduces the indirect object ('to/for'); داننده فرهنگیانم is a compound noun phrase 'the knowing/expert ones of my cultured people' (فرهنگیانم = 'my cultured/educated people'). سپار is an imperative/hortative verb ('entrust, give over') addressed to the addressee, so the whole segment means 'entrust (it) to my knowledgeable ones.'
segments.s584-g34: چو = 'when/if'; کارست is an archaic fused form ('کار است' = 'it is work/a task'); بیکار here is predicative ('idle/useless'); خوارم is 'despise/me treat as low' with the first‑person clitic -م; مدار is an archaic/poetic negative/imperative particle here meaning 'do not (do so)' so the clause reads 'when it is a task, do not despise me as idle.'
segments.s584-g35: بدو is a prepositional phrase 'to him' (to + 3rd‑person clitic), گفت is here the verb 'said' (word-order is inverted: 'Munḏir said to him'); منذر is the speaker's name (Munḏir). که‌ای is a vocative/exclamatory combination ('که ای' = 'O you ...') addressing the addressee; سرفراز is the vocative epithet 'exalted/victorious/honorable.'
segments.s584-g36: به marks the relation 'to/for'; فرهنگ = 'culture/learning' and نوزت = 'your newness/novelty' (the -ت is the 2nd‑person suffix). نیامد is the past negative 'did not come' and نیاز is 'need/longing/desire' (subject or predicate). Together: 'the need/appetite for culture has not come to your youth.'
segments.s584-g37: چو = 'when'; هنگام فرهنگ = 'the time/season of culture/learning'; باشد is the subjunctive/hortative copula 'be/let it be' (classical form), and ترا (t + ra split in the list) marks the second person ('for you'). The clause means 'when the time for learning arrives for you.'
segments.s584-g38: به دانایی = 'toward knowledge/wisdom' (به marks direction or aim); آهنگ = 'intention/aim' here ('let your aim be toward wisdom'); باشد ترا repeats the hortative/copular idea 'let it be for you.' In short: 'let your intention be directed to wisdom.'
segments.s584-g39: به ایوان = 'on/at the portico/veranda'; نمانم = first‑person negative of ماندن ('I will not remain' or 'I will not stay'); که here introduces the subordinate clause ('while/that/so that') and بازی کنی is a subjunctive/optative 'you play' (کنی = 2nd‑person present/subjunctive). Together: 'I will not stay on the portico while you play.'
segments.s584-g40: به بازی = 'by/with play'; همی is an archaic particle that intensifies or marks ongoing/habitual action (here 'indeed/by playing'); سرفرازی = 'exaltation/honor/pride' and کنی ('you do' in subjunctive/present) indicates the addressee's expected action—literally 'by playing you (think to) gain glory.'
segments.s584-g105: چو introduces a conditional ('if' / 'when') and governs the subordinate clause that follows. با تگ ('with a t(a)g') is an instrumental phrase: با + noun (تگ, a strap/girth) indicating the means. چنان پایدارش کنم contains the adjective چنان 'so/thus' modifying پایدارش ('make him steady') and the verb کنم (present subjunctive/regular present 'I make' / 'I will make'); the object suffix -ش refers back to the horse.
segments.s584-g106: به نوروز is a temporal phrase 'at/for Nowruz' (به marking time). با باد is another instrumental/associative phrase 'with the wind' (باد = wind). یارش 'his companion' is a possessive construction (یار + -ش). کنَم is the speaker's verb 'I make/do' continuing the conditional result begun in the previous segment.
segments.s584-g107: وگر is a conditional 'if / but if'. آزموده is a predicate/adjective meaning 'tried, proven, experienced' describing ستور. نباشد here is a negative copula/subjunctive form (classical Persian for 'is not' / 'be not'), linking آزموده to ستور; this is an archaic/poetic negative construction. ستور is the noun 'steed/horse' (the subject being described).
segments.s584-g108: نشاید is an impersonal/implicative modal meaning 'it is not fitting / one should not'. به تندی is an adverbial phrase 'in haste / quickly' (به + noun used adverbially). برو کرد زور is a small clause where برو is an exhortative/infinitival sense 'to go/run/urge on' (here 'urge to run'), کرد is the verbal root 'to do/make' and زور 'strength/force' is the instrument or manner — 'do with force/with forceful action.' The whole phrase expresses that forcing a raw horse to run fast is improper.
segments.s584-g109: به نعمان introduces the indirect object 'to Nu'mān' (the addressee). بفرمود is a formal/polite perfective reporting verb 'he said/commanded'. منذر is the vocative/name being addressed. که introduces the content clause (that/which). رو here is the verbal imperative/radical 'go' (classical poetic spelling coincides with the noun 'ro/face' in writing) and functions as the clause-initial imperative telling Munẓir to set out.
segments.s584-g110: فسیله گزین is an imperative sequence: گزین 'choose' governs its object فسیله. Here فسیله means a fresh/appropriate mount or a new pick (in context 'a fresh steed'); the exact lexical nuance is dialectal/poetic. از گله‌دار نو is a prepositional phrase 'from the herdsman, new/fresh' (از = from, گله‌دار = herdsman, نو = new/fresh), specifying the source of the mount.
segments.s584-g111: همه دشت is a noun phrase 'the whole plain' (همه = all/whole + دشت = plain). پیش سواران is a locative/genitive phrase 'in front of/the head of the riders' (پیش = before/in front of, سواران = riders). بگرد is an imperative verb 'search/look around' commanding action across that area.
segments.s584-g112: نگر is an imperative 'look!' or 'see!'. تا introduces a purpose/finite clause 'so that' or 'to the extent that' (here 'to see where...'). کجا is the interrogative 'where'. یابی is the present-subjunctive/imperative-form 'you may find / you find' (used here as part of the subordinate purpose clause). اسپ نبرد is a noun phrase 'a war-steed' (اسپ = horse, نبرد = battle/war used attributively).
segments.s584-g113: بشد is the past of شدن ('became'/'grew'); here it precedes the adjective تیز, so the clause reads literally 'he became swift.' نعمان is the subject (proper name) placed after the predicate — a common epic word order. صد اسپ آورید is a separate clause: صد (a numeral) precedes اسپ (horse), and آورید is a past/plural narrative form 'they brought' (third-person plural past in literary register).
segments.s584-g114: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' introducing the source group (اسپان, plural of اسپ). جنگی is a post‑nominal adjective qualifying those horses ('war‑/battle‑horses'). بسی is a quantifier meaning 'many'/'much' (here indicating quantity) and برگزید is the past tense verb 'he chose/selected,' whose subject is understood from the previous clause (Nuʿmān).
segments.s584-g115: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when.' The clause چو بهرام دید آن places the verb دید ('saw') before the demonstrative object آن ('that/him') — typical classical order: 'when Bahram saw that.' بیامد is the main past verb 'he came' and به دشت ('into the plain') marks direction/motion.
segments.s584-g116: چپ و راست is a paired directional phrase 'left and right.' پیچید is the past verb 'turned' or 'twisted.' the coordinating و links actions; چندی is an adverb meaning 'for a while' (duration), and بگشت is a narrative past of گشتن 'he turned/was about' — here describing that he rode around briefly. (Note: بگشت is an older narrative form built on گشتن.)
segments.s584-g117: هر اسپی که introduces a relative clause 'every horse that…' که is the relative pronoun 'which/that.' با باد همبر is a prepositional phrase meaning 'equal to the wind' or 'matching the wind' (همبر = peer/equal). بدی here is an archaic verbal/copular form meaning 'was' (classical variant of 'بود' used in heroic poetry) linking همبر to اسپی, so the clause reads 'every horse that was a match for the wind.'
segments.s584-g118: همه is 'all/everyone' referring back to the horses; زیر بهرام is the prepositional phrase 'under Bahram' meaning 'under his control / beneath him.' بی‌پر is a compound adjective literally 'without wings' (figuratively 'deprived of wing/strength' or 'disarmed'), and شدی is the past verb 'became'/'were made.' Together: 'all became wingless under Bahram.'
segments.s584-g119: برین‌گونه is an adverbial phrase 'in this manner' or 'thus.' تا here introduces the temporal/result clause 'until/when,' followed by برگزید 'he chose/selected.' اشقری is an adjective used substantively ('the scarlet one'), i.e. the color/epithet stands for the horse he selected.
segments.s584-g120: یکی normally means 'one' or 'a certain (one)' and functions as the noun article here ('one [horse]'). بادپایی is a compound adjective 'wind‑footed' = 'fleet‑footed/very fast.' گشاده‌بری is a poetic epithet literally 'wide‑carrying' (here describing a broad‑striding or open‑gaited horse); it functions adjectivally to complete the portrait of the chosen steed.
